story: I talked with my colleague about the upcoming project deadline and just knew he was feeling as overwhelmed as I was with the details piling up. Based on how I would react in his position, I assumed he wanted to push everything back too. When he said he was fine continuing, I still scheduled extra buffer time because my own stress levels made it clear what he must be experiencing internally.

Reviewing the medical trial results, even though most patients in similar past studies recovered without the new treatment, I focused on the few dramatic recoveries I saw recently. Those standout cases convinced me the drug was essential for everyone in this group despite the broader statistics not supporting it strongly.

At the betting shop I watched a few horses win after their previous strong performances. My friend picked a different one but I stuck with the hot streak because recent winners made it obvious which one would continue performing well today.

Choosing between the two vacation packages, the basic one seemed weak next to the deluxe with all extras. Once I picked the deluxe it felt like the perfect decision and I started noticing all the ways it was superior to justify the higher cost immediately.

After the family argument I remembered clearly how my prediction about the outcome was accurate all along. The way events unfolded matched what I had told everyone would happen exactly, confirming my judgment from the beginning.

In the ceramics studio I saw the new student struggle with basic forms and assumed they lacked dedication like others from similar backgrounds. My own quick progress made it easy to project that they weren't trying hard enough.
The sports team had won three games in a row so I bet heavily on them continuing the streak. Past patterns from other teams showed this momentum was likely to carry forward in the league.
